methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $9.2B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $7.5B.

data.narrative:
Saronic is a Series D defense and security company founded by Dino Mavrookas, currently valued at $9.25B post-money following $2.6B in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of ten comparables: two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private firms including Flock Safety, Chaos Industries, Skydio, True Anomaly, and Epirus.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression methodology, anchored to the $9.2B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.5x multiplier for Series D to next-stage advancement, implies a value of $13.9B. Peer-Median analysis across six same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables yielded a median valuation of $1.9B, with peer values ranging from $500M to $7.5B. The dispersion reflects material heterogeneity within defense-tech: Flock Safety at $7.5B (Series E) represents the upper bound, while Epirus at $1.4B (Series D) anchors the lower quartile.

Triangulation across both methods produces a range of $1.6B to $16.0B, with a median of $7.9B. The current $9.25B valuation sits above the peer median, reflecting either premium positioning or stage advancement embedded in recent fundraising. Key uncertainties include the peer set's heterogeneity in business model and growth trajectory, the applicability of the 1.5x stage multiplier to this specific company, and whether recent comparable rounds reflect market consensus or outliers.
